Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs' Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement***Redacted***
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RICHARD J. ZUROMSKI, Esq. of Seifert Zuromski LLP (Tel: 415-293-7966) for plaintiffs Robert Stang and Luigi Di Rocco;
Atty. WOODY WU, Esq. of SAC Attorneys, LLP (Tel: 408-436-0789) for defendants BRY1890 LLC, MRY101, LLC, Henry Yeap, and German Zhao;
Defendant JASON XU in pro per.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS ROBERT STANG and LUIGI DI RUOCCO's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion to enforce settlement agreement is denied in part and granted in part.
Plaintiffs and defendants settled a dispute over sale of a coffee bar. Per the agreement, the settlement sum was "allocated as follows: Jason Xu - 40%; Henry Yeap - 30%; German Zhao - 30%." Yeap and Zhao paid their allocations, but Xu did not pay his allocation.
Plaintiffs' motion looks to all three defendants for Xu's 40%, arguing that the settlement sum was "joint and several." However, the settlement agreement does not contain the term "joint and several," and instead allocates each of the three defendants a percentage of the settlement sum to pay. Plaintiffs further argue Civil Code 1660, which states: "A promise, made in the singular number, but executed by several persons, is presumed to be joint and several." However, no "singular number" exists here; rather, the settlement sum is allocated among the three payors in multiple numerical percentages.
Plaintiff's motion also seeks judgment as to a settlement agreement provision requiring defendants to return "control (to the extent they have control) over the domain name 'www.cofeebarsf.com' and all related social media accounts." Yeap and Zhao make satisfactory showings in these regards. Xu - who is no longer represented by counsel in this case, filed no papers and did not appear at hearing - made no showing.
In sum, judgment shall be entered against Xu for his 40% of the settlement sum and ordering him to return "control (to the extent [he has] control) over the domain name 'www.cofeebarsf.com' and all related social media accounts." (CCP 664.6.) No judgment shall be entered against Yeap or Zhao. No attorney fees are awarded.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Motion For Relief From And To Set Aside And Or Vacate The Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Complaint:

Plaintiffs Robert Stang and Luigi Di Ruocco's CCP 473 motion to set aside and/or vacate the dismissal of their complaint is granted. Plaintiffs move under the discretionary provisions of 473(b) or, in the alternative, its mandatory provisions. (Not. 2:3-7.) The motion is granted under the former, so the latter need not be reached. Plaintiffs are not lawyers; any neglect in monitoring former counsel's discovery failures was excusable. (See Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 740 ("a client should not be required to act as a hawklike inquisitor of his own counsel, nor perform incessant checking on counsel").) Further, California law strongly favors deciding cases on their merits. (Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389.)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Discovery Misuse
Off Calendar Aug-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. SUSAN BREED, ESQ. of Law Offices of Denise A. Leadbetter (Tel: 415-572-5015) appearing for plaintiffs/cross-defendants;
Atty. JOSEPH M. ECHEVARRIA, ESQ. of Law Offices of Sam X.J. Wu (Tel: 626-588-2388) appearing via CourtCall for defendants/cross-complainants Bry1890 LLC and Mry101 LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BRY1890 LLC AND MRY101 LLC's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Discovery Misuse.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in full. Defendants and Cross-Complainants Bry1890 LLC and MRY101 LLC's motion for terminating sanctions for discovery misuse is granted. As the report recites, over a period of more than 9 months, plaintiffs Robert Stang and Luigi Di Ruocco failed to respond to discovery requests properly served on them, ignored defendants' repeated communications requesting that they comply, failed to oppose six motions to compel, and then disregarded orders of the court granting those motions and requiring them to serve discovery responses and pay monetary sanctions. When plaintiffs finally did provide some written responses, just days before the hearing on defendants' motion, their responses were woefully deficient. Plaintiffs did not even bother to file a memorandum in opposition to defendants' motion for terminating sanctions, but instead filed only a brief declaration of counsel which offered absolutely no explanation for plaintiffs' failure to comply with their discovery obligations or even to respond to communications from opposing counsel, and instead improperly accused plaintiffs-without foundation-of propounding voluminous discovery that "seemed largely propounded primarily to exert the maximum burden on Defendants." Plaintiffs' unfounded speculation about defendants' motive for seeking discovery cannot by any stretch of the imagination justify their repeated failures to comply with their obligations under the Discovery Act and with the court's orders. Under the circumstances, the court agrees that plaintiffs' repeated failures to provide timely discovery responses and to comply with the court's orders constitute willful, repeated, and egregious misuse of the discovery process that warrant the imposition of terminating sanctions. (See, e.g., CREED-21 v. City of Wildomar (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 690, 701-703; Department of Forestry and Fire Protection v. Howell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 154, 197-198; Los Defensores, Inc. v. Gomez (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 377, 390-392.) The recommended sanctions are less harsh than those sought by defendants. The court finds that no less severe sanction would be effective to produce full compliance with the discovery rules or to ensure that defendants receive a fair trial. Accordingly, plaintiffs' complaint filed October 19, 2018 is dismissed with prejudice. Within 30 days of the date of this order, plaintiffs shall file code-compliant discovery responses, without objection, to all of defendants' discovery requests except those specifically pertaining to plaintiffs' complaint. Finally, within 30 days of the date of this order, plaintiffs and their counsel shall pay all sanctions previously awarded against them, in the amount of $2,400, and shall further pay to defendants the additional sum of $2,655, representing the reasonable attorney's fees incurred by defendants in bringing the motion.

The Court instructs defense counsel to e-mail a proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
